div.head: The Ambassador in France (Edge) to the Acting Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Paris, July 4, 1931—3 p.m.
[Received July 4—11:56 a.m.Telegram in two sections.]

div.p: 404. The following is the question discussed by Mr. Mellon with Mr. Mills at 2:30.

We are assured by M. Flandin, the Minister of Finance, that the French Government will agree to the 12-year repayment period if the American Government will state that if an agreement can be reached between France, Germany and the other interested powers on the continuation of the deliveries in kind for the year 1931–1932, it will interpose no objection.

We understand from M. Flandin that the total of these payments is 250 million reichsmarks but all but 120 million had already been contracted for and financed which means that the German budget in the coming year only would be affected to the extent of 30 million dollars which could be financed internally over a longer period by the Government of the Reich to its own national[s].

A favorable answer would remove all existing questions as per your 329Dated July 4, 2 a.m., p. 133. which are capable of settlement between the United States and France and make an agreement in principle possible tonight.
